refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses the phrase 'shall support achieving,' which is somewhat vague and could imply implementation aspects. Refining it to 'shall enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's functional capability without specifying how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single obligation: achieving the energy efficiency target defined by the customer. There are no multiple obligations or compound requirements to split further.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through measurement that the NGHE solution's energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) meets or is less than the customer-defined target (E_target) over the agreed evaluation period.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish a configurable energy efficiency target management system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Tight integration between energy monitoring and operational control modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and feedback control, which can be modularized separately from other system functions, supporting independence and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and reliable energy measurement is feasible within the system constraints
Operational policies allow for dynamic adjustments based on feedback
Energy efficiency targets are well-defined and stable over the evaluation period
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the feedback control mechanism as a separate DP to further enhance modularity and reduce coupling with other operational functions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Contains physical energy consumption but lacks direct control and monitoring logic; would cause diffusion to other modules.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy monitoring; would require significant diffusion.
Safety may use energy data but is not responsible for monitoring or control; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, as it centralizes energy management functions and minimizes diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface reliability for real-time data.
Network Link latency could affect feedback control responsiveness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing a mechanism to monitor and control energy usage in real time, which supports meeting the acceptance criteria of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on Telemetry interface reliability for real-time data may cause diffusion if data is not consistently available.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) with minimal diffusion (2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the energy_per_ton metric over the evaluation period using data collected and controlled within the NGHE Solution module, supported by Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and logical, as it centralizes energy management functions and feedback control, minimizing diffusion and enabling effective verification.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate risks related to Telemetry interface reliability and Network Link latency to ensure real-time data availability and control responsiveness.
Consider defining fallback or redundancy mechanisms for Telemetry data to reduce diffusion risk.
Document interface performance requirements explicitly to support verification and operational reliability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The DP directly supports the FR with clear verification feasibility and logical allocation to the NGHE Solution module, which shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion. Although there are identified diffusion risks related to Telemetry interface reliability and Network Link latency, these are noted as risks to monitor and mitigate rather than reasons to further decompose the FR. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here and focus on risk mitigation and verification rather than further sub-FR derivation.

revise.why: To incorporate explicit fallback or redundancy mechanisms for Telemetry data and document interface performance requirements to mitigate diffusion risks and support verification.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR for energy efficiency target achievement.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for this FR and DP.
Telemetry and Network Link interfaces are critical for real-time data availability and control responsiveness.
open_risks: Dependency on Telemetry interface reliability may cause diffusion if data is not consistently available.
Network Link latency could impact control responsiveness and verification feasibility.
evidence_needed: Performance data on Telemetry interface reliability and latency.
Verification results of energy_per_ton metric over evaluation period.
Documentation of fallback or redundancy mechanisms for Telemetry data.
